energyCalculator.calculateImpact.intro: This data modeling is by {rewiringAmerica}, an independent nonprofit that supports customers accessing electrification rebates and home energy upgrades provides a range of the impact of your selected upgrade on your energy bill, and emissions reductions estimates for your project.
energyCalculator.calculateImpact.section: Enter your household information to see potential energy bill changes and emissions reductions by upgrade type.
energyCalculator.calculateImpact.field.a: Enter your street address, city, state, and ZIP code.
energyCalculator.calculateImpact.field.w: Select your water heating fuel to see the impact of a water heater upgrade.
energyCalculator.calculateImpact.section: Select an upgrade option to learn more about how it works within a household.
energyCalculator.calculateImpact.upgrade: A heat pump is a single appliance with two modes, heating and cooling. By transferring heat (instead of creating it), heat pumps use less energy, making them more efficient than traditional systems.
Weatherization includes insulation, air sealing, and other improvements that reduce energy waste and make your home more comfortable year-round.
Combining a heat pump with weatherization maximizes energy savings by pairing efficient heating and cooling with a well-sealed, insulated home.
A heat pump water heater uses electricity to move heat from the surrounding air to heat water, using significantly less energy than traditional electric or gas water heaters.
